{{Infobox group
| name = Heralds
| introduced = Ghostwater
}}

''See also: [[Heralds Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Heralds are a powerful rank of [[sacred artists]] situated between [[Sages]] and [[Monarchs]]. Unlike [[Sages]], who focus their willpower outwardly to influence the world, Heralds focus their power inwardly to enhance their own physical and spiritual capabilities. This process results in the merging of their body and spirit into a single entity, allowing them to shift between physical and spiritual forms at will. While this inward focus makes them exceptionally robust, it also makes them less sensitive to subtle external powers and [[Icons]], which they are described as being unable to perceive. Heralds possess immense military and political influence. They can serve as guardians of civilization, such as those protecting the borders against [[dragons]] in the [[Wasteland]], or act as leaders and representatives for various sects and clans. Some Heralds are unaffiliated, while others hold significant positions within organizations like [[Redmoon Hall]] or [[Abyssal Palace]]. Despite their high status, Heralds are not immune to all forms of attack and generally avoid direct combat with one another, often bound by truces or pacts. Reaching the peak of this rank requires a massive accumulation of resources and brute force, serving as the final stepping stone before an artist can attempt to ascend to the rank of [[Monarch]].}}

== Ghostwater ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|Six great [[Heralds]] gathered to inspect the [[Ghostwater]] pocket world.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c7">''Ghostwater'', chapter 7</ref>}}

== Underlord ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|[[Heralds]] are a level of power or entity mentioned by [[Yerin]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c5">''Underlord'', chapter 5</ref>}}

== Uncrowned ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The [[Heralds]] dropped their veils.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c9">''Uncrowned'', chapter 9</ref>}}

== Wintersteel ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|Throughout the conflict, various Heralds expressed unusual observations regarding the [[Ninecloud team]]. They maintained a policy of avoiding open combat, though relocating large numbers of their kind required a significant cost. While they might allow a single [[Underlord]] to operate freely within their control, the Heralds would execute [[Fury]] and [[Mercy]] should [[Yerin]] suffer a defeat. These entities are only half-spirits who combine with their [[Remnants]], and they have historically been identified by their red badges. Ultimately, a victory for [[Mercy]] would serve to demonstrate to the Heralds that the [[Akura clan]] possesses greater strength than the gold [[dragons]].}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|Many [[Heralds]] have made strange comments about the [[Ninecloud team]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c3">''Wintersteel'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|A victory for [[Mercy]] would show the [[Heralds]] that the [[Akura clan]] is stronger than the gold [[dragons]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c16">''Wintersteel'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Heralds]] do not engage in open blows.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c18">''Wintersteel'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|Moving many [[Heralds]] has a cost.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Heralds]] would kill [[Fury]] and [[Mercy]] if [[Yerin]] loses.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c22">''Wintersteel'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Heralds]] might not interfere with one [[Underlord]] running around while in their grip.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c25">''Wintersteel'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Heralds]] are only half spirits.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c26">''Wintersteel'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Heralds]] combine with their [[Remnants]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c26" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Heralds]] historically wore badges of red.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c28">''Wintersteel'', chapter 28</ref>}}

== Bloodline ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Heralds]] focus their willpower inwardly to enhance their own power beyond all limitations.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c5">''Bloodline'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Infusing techniques with willpower is not the exclusive domain of [[Sages]] and [[Heralds]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c19">''Bloodline'', chapter 19</ref>}}

== Reaper ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The unaffiliated [[Heralds]] occasionally answer to [[Northstrider]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c2">''Reaper'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Heralds]] find it difficult to comprehend [[Sage]] techniques.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c7">''Reaper'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Heralds]] are peers to [[Eithan]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c21">''Reaper'', chapter 21</ref>}}

== Dreadgod ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|It is difficult to deal spiritual damage to [[Heralds]] such that they die without leaving a [[Remnant]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c15">''Dreadgod'', chapter 15</ref>}}

== Waybound ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Heralds]] are powerful entities that require willpower to fight.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c3">''Waybound'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Heralds]] are a level of power or stage in [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c32">''Waybound'', chapter 32</ref>}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Cradle nav}}

[[Category:Groups]]
[[Category:Ghostwater]]
[[Category:Underlord]]
[[Category:Uncrowned]]
[[Category:Wintersteel]]
[[Category:Bloodline]]
[[Category:Reaper]]
[[Category:Dreadgod]]
[[Category:Waybound]]
[[Category:Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle groups]]
